cases.next_action: Write one focused caller-visible test that submits a job, advances the deterministic scheduler, and uses wait_for(lambda: job.status == "complete", timeout, description); run it before implementation, make the smallest production change, then refactor only while green.
cases.proof: RED must be an observed failure because status never becomes complete, not a setup error. GREEN is the focused command passing through the public seam, followed by the nearest relevant group. After each refactor move, rerun focused proof and finish with the nearby group.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim asynchronous completion only after observed RED, GREEN, nearby validation, and recorded residual risk; do not claim TDD from after-the-fact proof or timing sleeps.

cases.next_action: Write a focused test that observes DEBOUNCE_STARTED, advances the fake clock to just before DEBOUNCE_INTERVAL and proves no SEARCH_REQUESTED, then advances the remainder and proves one request whose timestamp delta is exactly DEBOUNCE_INTERVAL.
cases.proof: Observe an expected behavioral RED, implement the smallest timer behavior, rerun to GREEN, then run the nearest relevant group; use the fake clock and event timestamps/count as the independent oracle.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim the exact timing behavior only, not general debounce correctness or real-clock reliability beyond the proved seam.

cases.next_action: Hand off to $diagnosing-bugs because the cause and trusted red-capable reproduction are uncertain.
cases.proof: Require diagnosis to return trusted regression proof or a decision-needed packet before entering a TDD mutation cycle.
cases.claim_boundary: Do not mutate production code or claim a TDD regression fix while the cause and red-capable reproduction remain uncertain.

cases.next_action: Repair the missing event-loop fixture or test setup and rerun the focused test.
cases.proof: RED is established only when the repaired test runs and fails for the expected missing or wrong behavior.
cases.claim_boundary: A setup error is not RED and supports no TDD or behavioral claim.

cases.next_action: Classify the focused failure as valid RED if it is the expected missing-behavior failure; preserve evidence that the nearby failure is documented, pre-existing, and unrelated. After implementation, rerun both focused and nearby commands.
cases.proof: The focused test may establish GREEN when it passes, but the nearby command and documented baseline comparison must disclose the unrelated failure as residual validation evidence.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim focused behavior GREEN only; do not claim full TDD completion or a clean relevant suite because GREEN's nearby-test gate remains unsatisfied.

cases.next_action: Preserve the correct retry-count assertion, reject weakening it, and keep the repository-owned scheduler real behind the tested interface. Reconsider the seam or use the smallest legitimate boundary substitute if an external boundary exists.
cases.proof: Require the correct assertion to pass through owned in-process code; any double must pass the fidelity gate and replace a real boundary adapter.
cases.claim_boundary: Do not claim success from a weakened oracle or from mocking away repository-owned behavior.

cases.next_action: Extend or replace the fake so it reproduces the consumed timeout contract, preferably running the same adapter contract against the fake and real or local implementation.
cases.proof: Require contract parity for the timeout failure mode; if no contract comparison is available, explicitly record the unverified fidelity risk and obtain separate boundary evidence.
cases.claim_boundary: The current fake proves successful receipt only; it cannot support a claim about consumed timeout behavior.

cases.next_action: Refactor only behavior-preserving cleanup inside the tracer-bullet slice, one meaningful move at a time, rerunning the focused test after each move and the nearest relevant group before return. Stop before the broader seam redesign and return it as residual follow-up evidence.
cases.proof: Focused and nearby tests must remain GREEN throughout permitted cleanup.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim only the bounded refactor performed under GREEN; do not widen scope, mutate a tracker, or claim the broader redesign is handled.

cases.next_action: Refuse the completion claim and return the work as incomplete until the minimum proof packet exists.
cases.proof: Require Source Trace; observed RED command/result and expected-failure reason; GREEN command/result; relevant nearby validation or a skipped reason; refactor result or none; and residual risk or blocker.
cases.claim_boundary: Expected RED alone proves neither implementation nor completion.
